title: AI Guardian Configuration
description: Configuration file for ai-guardian security tool that protects IDE AI agents from unauthorized tool access and security risks

permissions.description: Tool permissions configuration - WHERE THE RULES LIVE. Controls whether AI Guardian enforces tool permission rules and defines the permission rules for tools (Skills, MCP servers, built-in tools like Bash/Write/Read). Works with permissions_directories (auto-discovery feeds INTO this section). Default: Built-in tools ALLOW, Skills and MCP servers BLOCK unless explicitly allowed. NEW unified structure in v1.4.0.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

rules.description: Permission rules for tools (Skills, MCP servers, built-in tools like Bash/Write/Read). Default: Built-in tools ALLOW, Skills and MCP servers BLOCK unless explicitly allowed.

oneOf.description: Object format with allow/deny arrays (backward compatible)

deny.description: Directories to scan for deny patterns

allow.description: Directories to scan for allow patterns

oneOf.description: Array format with mode in each entry (NEW: mode field actually used)

permissions_directories.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Auto-discover tool permissions - HOW TO AUTO-POPULATE RULES. Scans directories/GitHub repos for permission files and merges discovered rules INTO permissions.rules. Data flow: scan directories → discover permission files → generate rules → merge into permissions.rules. NOT related to directory_rules (filesystem path access control). Most users should use remote_configs instead. Supports both object format (allow/deny arrays) and direct array format.

remote_configs.description: RECOMMENDED: Load policies from remote URLs (enterprise/team policies). This is the preferred way to manage permissions.

urls.description: List of remote configuration URLs to fetch

refresh_interval_hours.description: Hours between automatic refresh of remote configs

expire_after_hours.description: Hours until cached remote config expires

secret_scanning.description: Secret scanning with Gitleaks control (NEW in v1.4.0). Object with 'enabled' property that supports boolean (permanent) or time-based (temporary) formats. NOTE: Currently hardcoded to Gitleaks only - multi-engine support (engines field) planned for v2.0.0.

engines.description: RESERVED/FUTURE: List of secret scanning engines to use (planned for v2.0.0). Currently not implemented - Gitleaks is always used regardless of this setting. Future engines: gitleaks, trufflehog, detect-secrets, secretlint.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during secret scanning (NEW in v1.4.0). Useful for test fixtures, examples, and documentation with fake credentials. Supports wildcards: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char), ~ (home directory). Examples: '**/tests/fixtures/**', '**/examples/**/*.example.*', '**/.gitleaks.toml'

ignore_tools.description: Tool name patterns to skip during secret scanning (NEW in v1.4.0). Useful for tools that read test data or documentation. Supports wildcards: * (any chars), ? (single char). Examples: [] (scan all tools), specific use cases vary. Most users should use ignore_files instead for test fixtures.

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Enhanced secret detection patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.7.0: nested under secret_scanning). Configure this section to use enterprise patterns instead of Gitleaks defaults. Presence = enabled, null or absence = disabled (use defaults). Supports cascade/fallback: pattern server → project .gitleaks.toml → Gitleaks defaults.

oneOf.description: DEPRECATED: Use presence/absence of pattern_server section instead. Will be removed in v2.0.0.

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for patterns (appended to url)

warn_on_failure.description: Show warning when pattern server fails (auth errors, network errors, etc). Default: true. Set to false to suppress warnings.

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this pattern_server section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

token_env.description: Environment variable name containing auth token

token_file.description: Path to file containing auth token (supports ~ expansion)

pattern_server.description: DEPRECATED: Move to secret_scanning.pattern_server (v1.7.0+). This root-level location is supported for backward compatibility but will be removed in v2.0.0.

oneOf.description: DEPRECATED: Use presence/absence of pattern_server section instead

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for patterns (appended to url)

warn_on_failure.description: Show warning when pattern server fails (auth errors, network errors, etc). Default: true. Set to false to suppress warnings.

token_env.description: Environment variable name containing auth token

token_file.description: Path to file containing auth token (supports ~ expansion)

prompt_injection.description: Prompt injection detection configuration (NEW in v1.2.0, time-based support in v1.4.0). Protects against prompt injection attacks that try to manipulate AI behavior.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

action.description: Action on violation (NEW in v1.7.0): 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows execution, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

detector.description: Detection method: heuristic (local patterns, <1ms), rebuff/llm-guard (ML-based, requires setup)

sensitivity.description: Detection sensitivity: low (obvious attacks only), medium (balanced), high (more aggressive)

allowlist_patterns.description: Regex patterns to ignore (for false positives). Supports time-based patterns.

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during prompt injection detection (NEW in v1.4.0). Supports wildcards: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char), ~ (home directory). Example: '**/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md' to ignore all skill documentation files.

ignore_tools.description: Tool name patterns to skip during prompt injection detection (NEW in v1.4.0). Supports wildcards: * (any chars), ? (single char). Examples: 'Skill:code-review' (ignore specific skill), 'Skill:*' or 'Skill' (ignore all skills), 'mcp__*' (ignore all MCP tools). Useful for tools that legitimately read documentation containing example attack patterns.

oneOf.description: DEPRECATED: Array format. Use object format with action field instead.

Filesystem path access control - Controls which PATHS can be accessed/read. Order-based directory access control rules (NEW in v1.6.0). Rules are evaluated sequentially with last match winning. Supports flexible allow/deny patterns for enterprise skill allowlists and path restrictions. COMPLETELY SEPARATE from permissions_directories (which auto-discovers TOOL permissions, not path restrictions). Common confusion: permissions_directories = where to find tool permission config, directory_rules = which paths to protect.

action.description: Action on violation: 'block' prevents access (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows access, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning. Applies to ALL rules.

rules.description: List of directory access rules evaluated in order (last match wins)

mode.description: Access mode: 'allow' permits access, 'deny' blocks access

paths.description: Directory paths for this rule. Supports ~ expansion, ** for recursive, * for single level.

directory_exclusions.description: DEPRECATED: Use directory_rules instead. Filesystem path access control - Controls which PATHS can be accessed/read. NOT related to permissions_directories (which auto-discovers TOOL permissions). Directory exclusions can override .ai-read-deny blocking (v1.5.0). Automatically converted to directory_rules internally.

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

paths.description: Directory paths to exclude from .ai-read-deny blocking (exclusions override deny markers). Supports ~ expansion, ** for recursive, * for single level. Ideal for enterprise skill allowlists.

permission_rule.description: Permission rule defining allow/deny patterns for a specific tool matcher

mode.description: Permission mode: 'allow' creates an allow-list, 'deny' creates a deny-list

patterns.description: List of patterns to allow/deny. Supports simple strings or time-based objects with valid_until.

action.description: Action on violation (NEW in v1.7.0): 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows execution, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot add or modify rules for this matcher. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

time_based_pattern.description: Pattern with optional time-based expiration (NEW in v1.3.0)

valid_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC (e.g., '2026-04-13T12:00:00Z'). Pattern expires after this time.

time_based_feature.description: Feature toggle with optional time-based temporary disabling (NEW in v1.4.0)

disabled_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC (e.g., '2026-04-13T18:00:00Z'). Feature override expires after this time.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the temporary change

directory_entry.description: Directory to scan for auto-discovery of tool permissions (NOT filesystem path blocking). Scans directory or GitHub repo for permission files and generates tool permission rules. Purpose: Auto-populate permissions.rules, not block directories. In object format (allow/deny arrays), mode is determined by which array the entry is in. In array format, mode defaults to 'allow' if not specified.

matcher.description: Tool type to apply discovered patterns to (e.g., 'Skill', 'mcp__*'). Defaults to 'Skill' if not specified.

mode.description: Permission mode for discovered patterns. Only used in array format; ignored in object format (allow/deny arrays). Defaults to 'allow'.

url.description: Local directory path or GitHub URL to scan

token_env.description: Environment variable containing GitHub token (for private repos)

remote_url_entry.description: Remote configuration URL with optional settings

url.description: Remote configuration URL (https://) or local file path

enabled.description: Whether this remote config is enabled

token_env.description: Environment variable containing auth token for remote URL
